query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Beau', 'A frequent or attentive male companion', 'Her beau became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Becalm', 'To make calm or still, make quiet, calm', 'Over time, the community learned to becalm its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beck', 'A stream or small river', 'The essay highlights the beck of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bedaub', 'To smear upon, to soil', 'The author hoped the new policy would bedaub tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bedeck', 'To deck, ornament, or adorn, to grace', 'The author hoped the new policy would bedeck tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bedlam', 'A place or situation of chaotic uproar', 'Her bedlam became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bedraggle', 'To make something wet and limp', 'Critics argued that the plan might bedraggle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Befog', 'To envelop in fog', 'Over time, the community learned to befog its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Befriend', 'To make a friend of', 'The author hoped the new policy would befriend tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Befuddle', 'To perplex or confuse (someone)', 'Critics argued that the plan might befuddle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beget', 'To cause, to produce', 'Critics argued that the plan might beget the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Begrudge', 'To be reluctant', 'Over time, the community learned to begrudge its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beguile', 'To charm or enchant deceptively', 'Critics argued that the plan might beguile the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Behemoth', 'A huge or monstrous creature', 'Scholars noted a striking behemoth between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Behest', 'To promise, vow', 'The author hoped the new policy would behest tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Behold', 'To see or observe something, often with a sense of wonder or amazement', 'The author hoped the new policy would behold tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Belabor', 'To argue or explain in excessive detail', 'The author hoped the new policy would belabor tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Belay', 'To surround, environ, enclose', 'Over time, the community learned to belay its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beleaguer', 'To lay siege to', 'The author hoped the new policy would beleaguer tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Belie', 'To fail to give a true impression of', 'Over time, the community learned to belie its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Belittle', 'Make seem unimportant', 'Scholars noted a striking belittle between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Belle', 'An attractive woman', 'The essay highlights the belle of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bellicose', 'Demonstrating aggression or a willingness to fight', '“His bellicose remarks escalated the argument.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Belligerent', 'Hostile and aggressive', 'What seemed belligerent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bellwether', 'The leading sheep of a flock, usually wearing a bell', 'The essay highlights the bellwether of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bemoan', 'To complain about, to dismay or worry about something', 'Critics argued that the plan might bemoan the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bemus', 'Confused or bewildered; deeply absorbed in thought', 'The essay highlights the bemus of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bemuse', 'To confuse or bewilder', 'Critics argued that the plan might bemuse the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Benediction', 'The utterance or bestowing of a blessing', 'The essay highlights the benediction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Benefactor', 'Somebody who gives one a gift', 'The essay highlights the benefactor of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Benefice', 'To bestow a benefice upon', 'The author hoped the new policy would benefice tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beneficial', 'In a way that produces good results', '“The changes were implemented beneficially for all employees.”', 'adverb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beneficiary', 'One who benefits or receives an advantage', 'The essay highlights the beneficiary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Benefit', 'To be or to provide a benefit to', 'Critics argued that the plan might benefit the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Benevolent', 'Well-meaning and kindly', '“The benevolent organization provided food and shelter for the needy.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Benign', 'Gentle and kind; not harmful', 'The essay highlights the benign of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Benison', 'A blessing, benediction', 'Her benison became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bent', 'Curved, crooked', 'What seemed bent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bequeath', 'To pass or to leave something over to someone else', 'Over time, the community learned to bequeath its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Berate', 'To scold or criticize angrily', 'Critics argued that the plan might berate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bereave', 'To deprive by or as if by violence, rob, strip', 'Critics argued that the plan might bereave the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bereft', 'Deprived', 'Her bereft became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beret', 'A type of round, brimless cap with a soft top and a headband', 'Her beret became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Berserk', 'Furiously violent or out of control', 'Her berserk became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Berth', 'To bring (a ship or vehicle) into its berth', 'Over time, the community learned to berth its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beseech', 'To beg or implore', 'The author hoped the new policy would beseech tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Beset', 'To surround or hem in', 'The author hoped the new policy would beset tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Besmear', 'To smear over, sully', 'Over time, the community learned to besmear its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Besmirch', 'To make dirty, to soil', 'The author hoped the new policy would besmirch tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bestial', 'Beast-like', 'Her bestial became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
